reasoning: The FRED data indicates an upward trend in the 10-year TIPS yield over the past six weeks, rising from 1.68 in October to 1.94 by early January 2026. The built-in linear model estimates a 67-68% probability of the yield exceeding 1.94 by Jan 11 due to short-term momentum. I anchor my shortest-horizon prediction near this value, at 65%. However, interest rates and yields also exhibit random walk properties with mean reversion over longer periods. As the forecast horizon extends to February 3 and April 4, macroeconomic uncertainty (e.g., changes in Fed policy or inflation expectations) naturally increases noise, diluting the impact of the current short-term trend. Therefore, in accordance with the principle that probabilities for more distant dates should revert towards 0.50, I scale the probabilities down to 58% for the 1-month horizon and 53% for the 3-month horizon.
